feedback.text: I would like to emphasize the importance of fostering fair competition and open ecosystems in the mobile smartphone market. Linux-based smartphones represent a crucial avenue for advancing user freedom, privacy, and innovation by offering a truly open alternative to dominant proprietary mobile operating systems. However, the current market is heavily skewed by practices that favor closed platforms through restrictive contracts, proprietary drivers, and ecosystem lock-ins. These barriers inhibit the development and adoption of pure Linux smartphones and limit consumer choice. I strongly support the DSAs objectives to prevent unfair commercial practices and antitrust behaviors that entrench dominant players. Ensuring transparency, interoperability, and openness in mobile ecosystems aligns with the goals of the DSA to promote fair market conditions and empower users with sovereignty over their devices and data. Specifically, I encourage the Commission to prioritize measures that: - Prevent practices by dominant platform providers that restrict device compatibility or pre-install monopolistic app ecosystems. - Promote the use of open standards and open-source drivers to enable hardware compatibility for alternative OS platforms like Linux. - Support developers and manufacturers in creating interoperable and sustainable ecosystems outside the traditional walled gardens. - Foster consumer awareness and access to open alternatives as part of digital sovereignty initiatives. The success of the Digital Services Act in creating a fair and competitive digital single market depends on recognizing and supporting such open technology initiatives. Linux-based smartphones exemplify the values of openness, user empowerment, and innovation that the EU should champion.
